reason: Genuine intro prose framing Jac's Python interoperability story.
Concrete code example showing how to import Jac modules from Python.
Bare horizontal-rule separator with no content.
Jac-to-Python type mapping table is useful reference content.
Introduces the report statement, the core mechanism for getting data out of walker traversals.
Framing prose explaining why walker response pattern choice matters.
Nav-only related-links block with no instructional content.
Bare horizontal-rule separator with no content.
Core spawn/report semantics: explains the .reports array as the walker's multi-shot return channel.
Useful behavioral note about report's stdout side effect.
Minimal canonical example of spawn plus report ordering into .reports.
Solid guidance on declaring typed reports as the default contract.
Explains the list[any] fallback and its downstream typing cost.
Short but real intro prose for the single-typed-report pattern.
Rich worked example exercising visit, here, disengage, spawn, and typed reports together.
Clarifies that reports is an ordinary has field with a default.
Explains the non-obvious list[list[T]] shape for collection walkers.
Code example for the single-shot typed collection pattern.
Concrete gotcha: missing default makes reports a required spawn parameter (E1050).
Header sentence that sets up the type-checker rules that follow.
Specifies the two type-checking rules enforced on the reports channel.
Explains the untyped fallback and strict-any consequence; complements rather than duplicates earlier chunk.
Documents the as-cast escape hatch for third-party walkers.
Section header only, no prose or code.
One-sentence intro to the recommended single-report pattern.
Canonical accumulator-pattern example combining visit, here, walker state, and Root exit reporting.
Brief but genuine when-to-use guidance for the pattern.
Names and explains the accumulator pattern, the standard graph-traversal collection model.
Three-stage traversal breakdown supporting the accumulator explanation.
Core walker-lifecycle semantics: when the with Root exit ability fires relative to the visit queue.
Practical tip on consuming the accumulator result from client code.
Single cross-reference sentence to a tutorial, nav-only with no content.
Intro sentence for the report-per-node pattern.
Typed search-walker example showing per-match reporting.
When-to-use guidance for search and filter walkers.
Intro sentence for the operation-plus-result pattern.
CRUD example including ++> edge creation and typed report of the created node.
When-to-use guidance for mutation walkers.
Introduces the nested walker spawning pattern and its rationale.
Code example for passing data between nested walkers via has attributes.
Important caveat about inner-walker reports being inaccessible from parent context.
Intro sentence for the multiple-reports pattern.
Example of multi-report walkers producing detail plus summary.
When-to-use guidance for multi-report operations.
Introduces defensive handling of empty reports.
Practical safe-access idioms for consuming .reports.
Introduces the response object structure section.
Documents the response object's available properties; distinct enough from the earlier minimal example.
Dense best-practices list consolidating typing, reporting, and hydration guidance.
Bare Anti-Patterns section header with no accompanying content.
